# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Create a simple GitHub Actions workflow for our application CI. On every push, install dependencies, run unit tests, build the application, and deploy to our staging environment. Focus on speed - we want fast feedback for developers. We'll add security scanning later.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI
on: [push]
jobs:
  build_and_deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v2
        with:
          node-version: 14

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run unit tests
        run: npm test

      - name: Build the application
        run: npm run build

      - name: Deploy to staging environment
        uses: addison-law/ssh-action@v2.16.0
        with:
          private_key: ${{ secrets.SSH_PRIVATE_KEY }}
          host: staging.example.com
          username: deploy
          port: 22
          script: |
            cd /path/to/your/project
            git pull origin `git rev-parse HEAD`
            cp -R ./dist/* /var/www/html/
